Coming vs. the Day of the Lord

Next The 59 "One Another" Commands

Now we request you, brethren, with regard to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to Him...

Date: 20 Jul 2025
Series: BibleStudy
Tags:
Day of the LordComing of the Lord
Status: published

We Are Not Destined for Wrath

Section titled “We Are Not Destined for Wrath”

1 Thessalonians 1:10
”and to wait for His Son from heaven, whom He raised from the dead, that is, Jesus who rescues us from the wrath to come.”

Revelation 3:10
”Because you have kept My word of perseverance, I also will keep you from the hour of the testing, that hour which is about to come upon the whole world, to test those who live on the earth.”

1 Thessalonians 5:9
”For God has not destined us for wrath, but for obtaining salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.”

Romans 5:9
”Much more then, having now been justified by His blood, we shall be saved from the wrath of God through Him.”

John 3:36
”The one who believes in the Son has eternal life; but the one who does not obey the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”

Ephesians 2:3-5
”Among them we too all previously lived in the lusts of our flesh… but God, being rich in mercy… made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved).”

Colossians 3:6-7
”For it is because of these things that the wrath of God is coming upon the sons of disobedience…”

Isaiah 26:20-21
”Come, My people, enter your rooms… until indignation runs its course.”

Romans 8:1
”Therefore there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.”

The Coming of the Lord (Precedes the Day)

Section titled “The Coming of the Lord (Precedes the Day)”

1 Thessalonians 4:13-18
”But we do not want you to be uninformed, brethren… Then we who are alive and remain will be caught up… and so we shall always be with the Lord.”

John 14:1-3
”Do not let your heart be troubled… I will come again and receive you to Myself…”

1 Corinthians 15:51-57
”Behold, I tell you a mystery; we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed…”

The Day of the Lord

Section titled “The Day of the Lord”

1 Thessalonians 5:1-7
”For you yourselves know full well that the day of the Lord will come just like a thief in the night…”

2 Thessalonians 2:1-8
”Let no one in any way deceive you… the man of lawlessness is revealed…”

What Happens at the Rapture?

Section titled “What Happens at the Rapture?”

Hebrews 11:39-40
”All these, having gained approval through their faith, did not receive what was promised…”

Judgment of Believers (Bema Seat)

Section titled “Judgment of Believers (Bema Seat)”

2 Corinthians 5:9-10
”For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ…”

1 Corinthians 4:5
”Then each one’s praise will come to him from God.”

Revelation 22:12
”Behold, I am coming quickly, and My reward is with Me…”

Crowns

Section titled “Crowns”
  • Imperishable Crown — 1 Corinthians 9:24-25
  • Crown of Righteousness — 2 Timothy 4:8
  • Crown of Life — James 1:12
  • Crown of Glory — 1 Peter 5:4

Thrones and Authority

Section titled “Thrones and Authority”

Luke 22:28-30
”You are those who have stood by Me… I assign to you a kingdom…”

Revelation 20:4
”Then I saw thrones, and they sat on them…”

Wedding Pattern (Jewish Context)

Section titled “Wedding Pattern (Jewish Context)”
  • Bridegroom comes for the bride
  • Returns to the Father’s house
  • Wedding period (typologically seven days)
  • Returns with the bride

Feast Timeline (Prophetic Picture)

Section titled “Feast Timeline (Prophetic Picture)”
  • Feast of Trumpets — Gathering
  • Day of Atonement (Yom Kippur) — Judgment / Tribulation
  • Feast of Tabernacles — Kingdom dwelling

Pretribulation Argument Summary

Section titled “Pretribulation Argument Summary”
  • Coming of the Lord → For His church (comfort, gathering)
  • Day of the Lord → Judgment upon the world

Revelation 4 Observations

Section titled “Revelation 4 Observations”
  • 24 Elders → Representation of redeemed people
  • White Garments → Imputed righteousness
  • Crowns → Rewards
  • Seven Spirits → Fullness of the Holy Spirit (Isaiah 11:2)

Coming vs. Day of the Lord (Summary)

Section titled “Coming vs. Day of the Lord (Summary)”
Coming of the LordDay of the Lord
For believersJudgment on unbelievers
ComfortDestruction
ImminentPreceded by signs
GatheringWrath

Day of the Lord (Tribulation Context)

Section titled “Day of the Lord (Tribulation Context)”

2 Thessalonians 1:6-10
”God will repay with affliction those who afflict you…”

Feast of Booths (Tabernacles)

Section titled “Feast of Booths (Tabernacles)”

Biblical Background

Section titled “Biblical Background”
  • Wilderness remembrance — Leviticus 23:42-43
  • Harvest festival — Exodus 23:16; Leviticus 23:39

Key Observances

Section titled “Key Observances”
  • Dwelling in booths
  • Lulav and Etrog — Leviticus 23:40

New Testament Fulfillment

Section titled “New Testament Fulfillment”

John 7:37
”If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.”

Eschatological Fulfillment

Section titled “Eschatological Fulfillment”

Zechariah 14:16-19
”Then it will come about that any who are left of all the nations… will go up from year to year to worship the King…”

Final Notes

Section titled “Final Notes”
  • The Church is not appointed to wrath
  • The Rapture is distinct from the Day of the Lord
  • The prophetic feasts provide a framework for understanding redemptive history
